stories.headline: PJM's reopened interconnection queue draws 811 projects totaling 220 GW — gas leads at 106 GW
stories.summary: PJM announced 811 generation projects totaling 220 GW applied to Cycle 1 of its reformed queue: 105.8 GW gas, 66.5 GW storage, 17.9 GW nuclear, 14.8 GW solar; review runs 1–2 years and reshapes the next decade of PJM capacity.

stories.headline: PA Senate committee hears grid issues are getting worse; PJM 'backstop' procurement called critical
stories.summary: A PA Senate committee took testimony on worsening grid reliability, the 220-GW PJM queue, and PJM's proposed backstop procurement to make data centers pay for new generation rather than shifting cost to ratepayers.

stories.headline: Vineyard Wind contracts switch on, easing New England electricity prices
stories.summary: Vineyard Wind's commercial power purchase agreements have begun delivering, the project's first stabilizing impact on regional rates and a counterpoint to the wave of canceled offshore wind projects further south.

stories.headline: MISO capacity prices fall as new supply outpaces demand growth
stories.summary: MISO's latest capacity auction cleared lower as new supply additions outpaced load growth — a notable divergence from PJM's record $329.17/MW-day 2026/27 clearing price and a useful comparator for PA/NJ co-op planners.

stories.headline: Garrity and Shapiro split on broadband and federal program cuts in PA gubernatorial race
stories.summary: Spotlight PA contrasts Treasurer Stacy Garrity and Gov. Josh Shapiro on rural broadband deployment and how to absorb federal program cuts — directly relevant to co-op pole-attachment broadband and RUS-funded service.

stories.headline: PA-01: Fitzpatrick attacks Harvie over open-primaries stance
stories.summary: Rep. Brian Fitzpatrick (R-Pa.) called Bucks County commissioner Bob Harvie's opposition to open primaries an 'insult to independent voters,' opening the general-election attack lines in PA's most competitive House race.

stories.headline: Advocates push 'skill games' tax as lottery losses cut PA senior programs
stories.summary: Pennsylvania Association of Area Agencies on Aging warned cuts to senior programs will worsen unless Harrisburg replaces declining Lottery revenue, putting renewed pressure on a skill-games tax in the General Assembly.

stories.headline: PA House passes bill allowing PIAA to split public and private playoffs
stories.summary: The state House passed HB 41 authorizing the PIAA to separate boundary (public) and non-boundary (private/charter/Catholic) schools into different postseason brackets, ending a years-long fairness fight.

stories.headline: SCOTUS guts VRA Section 2; analysts see 1–4 GOP House seats this cycle, more in 2028
stories.summary: Louisiana v. Callais sharply curtailed race-based redistricting under VRA Section 2; Cook's Matthew Klein and Sabato's J. Miles Coleman estimate 1–4 GOP seats this fall, with bigger 2028/2030 implications and likely blue-state retaliation.

stories.headline: FISA Section 702 expires today; Thune rejects Johnson's House deal, sends 45-day patch
stories.summary: Senate Majority Leader John Thune rejected Speaker Mike Johnson's FISA package after the House loaded it with a CBDC ban, and is jamming the House with a 45-day Section 702 extension before authority lapses Thursday.

stories.headline: House adopts Senate budget resolution 215-211 teeing up ICE/Border Patrol reconciliation
stories.summary: After five hours of holding the vote open, House Republicans cleared the budget resolution late Wednesday, putting GOP leaders on a tight track to a final reconciliation bill by Trump's June 1 deadline.

stories.headline: Hegseth and Caine face Senate Armed Services on Iran war as cost estimates hit $40–50B
stories.summary: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Joint Chiefs Chair Gen. Dan Caine testify at 11 a.m. before SASC; Pentagon cited $25B but CNN sources peg the all-in cost at $40–50B once Mideast base repairs are included.

stories.headline: Powell-Tillis fight ends as DOJ closes probe; Warsh on track at Fed despite policy dissent
stories.summary: Sen. Thom Tillis (R-N.C.) cleared Kevin Warsh's path after DOJ ended its Powell probe, but multiple regional Fed presidents publicly oppose the rate-cut posture — the deepest policy split since 1992 — ahead of today's Q1 GDP print at 8:30 a.m.

stories.headline: Flyers eliminate Penguins in OT, 1-0, win first-round series 4-2
stories.summary: Cam York's blue-line shot through a Noah Cates screen with 2:28 left in OT ended Pittsburgh's season; Arturs Silovs stopped 31 shots for the Pens, who blew a chance to become the fifth team ever to erase a 3-0 series deficit.

stories.headline: Cardinals score 4 in 9th to beat Pirates 5-4; Bucs drop to 16-16
stories.summary: Pedro Pages and JJ Wetherholt hit back-to-back HRs off Dennis Santana to tie it, and Jose Fermin's two-run double scored Victor Scott for the lead in a four-run ninth as Pittsburgh's losing streak hit three.

stories.headline: Steelers 2026 draft class headlined by OT Iheanachor, WR Bernard, QB Allar
stories.summary: Pittsburgh took Arizona State OT Max Iheanachor at No. 21, traded up for Alabama WR Germie Bernard at 47, and used a third on Penn State QB Drew Allar — graders split, with most calling Allar an early reach.
